summary: DeVault argues that the tech sector is central to the rise of contemporary fascism, particularly in the United States, and that tech workers have both the ability and the obligation to resist. He identifies Palantir's surveillance infrastructure and DOGE's technocratic agenda as key instruments of authoritarian power. The post calls for collective action through unionization, workplace sabotage, and mutual support among tech workers. He emphasizes that solidarity and safety in numbers are essential, urging workers to abandon American individualism. DeVault acknowledges the fear involved but insists that inaction also carries risks, and asks readers to decide where they will draw the line.
key_points: The tech sector is the enabling infrastructure of contemporary fascism, from DOGE's performative disruption to Palantir's surveillance database
Tech workers hold unique leverage because the regime's digital instruments depend on their labor
Unionization is the most important collective action tech workers can take to compel employers to resist
Individual sabotage tactics — dropping databases, overlooking bugs, becoming single points of failure — are viable for those with higher risk tolerance
Solidarity and mutual support are the foundation of resistance: don't go it alone, and protect those who take direct action
money_quotes: The enemy wants you to be overwhelmed and depressed, to feel like the situation is out of your control.
Where DOGE is hilariously tragic, Palantir is nightmarishly effective.
It's clear that the regime will be digital.
There's safety in numbers. It is of the utmost importance that we dispense with American individualism and join hands with our allies to resist as one.
If tech workers unionize then we can compel our employers – this regime's instruments of fascist power – to resist also.
Make yourself a single point of failure, then fail.
Big tech has become the problem, and it's time for tech workers to be a part of the solution.
Don't let them shift your private goalposts until the frog is well and truly boiled to death.
key_insight: Tech workers are uniquely positioned to resist technocratic authoritarianism because the regime's digital power depends entirely on their labor, making collective action and workplace resistance both possible and morally imperative.
